assumptions: The question concerns designation of the foreign exchange exposure in consolidated financial statements under IFRS hedge accounting.
The intragroup dividend has resulted in a recognized receivable, ie a monetary item.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies recognized assets and liabilities as possible hedged items, making the fair value hedge model a core peer hedge accounting approach for a recognized foreign-currency receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately identifies forecast transactions as hedged items, so the cash flow hedge model is a distinct peer approach relevant to foreign-currency intragroup exposures in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 establish a separate hedge accounting model for foreign exchange risk on a net investment in a foreign operation.
